title: UNION and UNION ALL — combining two queries vertically
description: UNION and UNION ALL — combining two queries vertically. Key topics: UNION ALL: Combines results of two SELECTs (duplicates allowed). UNION:     Same as UNION ALL but removes duplicates (uses sort). Both queries must have the SAME NUMBER of columns with.

sections.title: Key Learning Points
sections.description:
◆UNION ALL: Combines results of two SELECTs (duplicates allowed)
◆UNION: Same as UNION ALL but removes duplicates (uses sort)
◆Both queries must have the SAME NUMBER of columns with
◆Column names are taken from the FIRST SELECT
◆UNION ALL is FASTER than UNION because it doesn't sort for dedup
◆ORDER BY sorts the final combined result (applied at the end)
◆@Semantics annotations on UNION fields apply to the combined result.

sections.title: CDS Source Code

content:
Key Learning Points

◆UNION ALL: Combines results of two SELECTs (duplicates allowed)
◆UNION: Same as UNION ALL but removes duplicates (uses sort)
◆Both queries must have the SAME NUMBER of columns with
◆Column names are taken from the FIRST SELECT
◆UNION ALL is FASTER than UNION because it doesn't sort for dedup
◆ORDER BY sorts the final combined result (applied at the end)
◆@Semantics annotations on UNION fields apply to the combined result.

CDS Source Code

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZPRAC_SQL_UNION'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Practice CDS - UNION ALL combining Travel + Booking'
define view ZPRAC_CDS_UNION
  as select from /DMO/I_Travel_D

{
  key TravelID               as Identifier,
      @Semantics.date: true
      BeginDate              as EventDate,
      @Semantics.date: true
      EndDate                as EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      TotalPrice             as Amount,
      Description            as Text,
      'Travel'               as RecordType,
      TravelUUID             as SourceUUID
}

union all

  select from /DMO/I_Booking_D
{
  key BookingID              as Identifier,
      @Semantics.date: true
      BookingDate            as EventDate,
      @Semantics.date: true
      FlightDate             as EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      FlightPrice            as Amount,
      ''                     as Text,
      'Booking'              as RecordType,
      BookingUUID            as SourceUUID
}
details:
COMPATIBLE data types

Each SELECT's column type must match; annotations use the first

SELECT's field names.

USE CASE:

Combine Travel and Booking into a single timeline of events.

Travel is the "header", Booking is the "item".

A RecordType discriminator identifies which kind each row is.
topic: Set operations — UNION / UNION ALL / INTERSECT / EXCEPT
